new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient, you must first search for Immunization resources using the patient ID, then check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must create a ServiceRequest (not a MedicationRequest) for a COVID-19 booster with CPT code 91320, and your final output must include both the date of the last immunization (or a statement that none was found) and confirmation that a new ServiceRequest was created, rather than incorrectly assuming no records exist without searching or ordering a MedicationRequest instead of a ServiceRequest.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources for DVT prophylaxis (e.g., heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, then count them; if exactly one active order exists, you must leave it as-is regardless of the frequency or dose specified, and not create a new order or discontinue the existing one, and your final output must confirm the single order was found rather than continuing to search additional pages or attempting to modify the order.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient; if the search returns zero results, do not conclude that no catheter exists—instead, verify the search was performed correctly and consider that the catheter might be documented differently, and if a procedure is found with a performedDateTime more than 48 hours before now, then search for a removal ServiceRequest using a removal-specific code (such as NUR1374) and only create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter if no such removal order exists.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient, you must first search for Immunization resources using the patient ID, then check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must create a ServiceRequest (not a MedicationRequest) for a COVID-19 booster with CPT code 91320, and your final output must include both the date of the last immunization (or a statement that none was found) and confirmation that a new ServiceRequest was created, rather than incorrectly creating a MedicationRequest or skipping the creation step.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources using the patient ID and specifically filter for opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, morphine, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone) — do not incorrectly conclude that no opioid orders exist by only reviewing non-opioid medications like alectinib or levothyroxine; you must thoroughly examine all medication codes in the search results and follow pagination links to retrieve all pages before concluding no active opioid orders are present.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID; if the search returns zero results, you must report that no study was found and then create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', rather than simply returning "No CT Abdomen ImagingStudy found" and stopping without creating the required order.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, and if the search returns zero results, you must report that no study was found AND then create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', ensuring your final output includes both the statement that no study was found and the new order (e.g., FINISH(["No CT Abdomen study found", "ServiceRequest created"])), rather than simply returning "No CT Abdomen ImagingStudy found" and stopping without creating the required order.
